details.text: Derivative: تُطِعِ from أَطاعَ – يُطِيعُ which means to obey and can also implie to allow yourself to be influenced (by someone or something).

Derivative: زَاغَتِ from زَاغَ means to turn aside, to turn away. In this context زَاغَتِ الْأَبْصَارُ (Their eyes shifted) it can mean that their eyes lost focus and became glazed over due to fear or that they turned their vision from everything apart from the enemy that was approaching from both sides.

Derivative: الظُّنُونَا refers to negative thoughts and assumptions. The extra أَلِف (Alif) at the end of الظُّنُونَا is for emphasis and exaggeration (Mubaalaghah) to emphasise all the types of thoughts and assumptions that were going through the minds of the Muslims.
- “And the hearts reached the throats” is an expression to highlight the extreme fear and terror experienced. When the hearts reach the throat implies being too scared to even speak.
Derivative: غُرُورًا is a deception, delusion or con i.e., like a fake promise.

- “Those who have a disease in their hearts” are those who have doubts in Islam but have not reached the level of الْمُنَافِقُونَ (The hypocrites) who deceptively declare and show belief in Islam whilst really being disbelievers.
Derivative: مُقَامَ means standing and place to stay. لَا مُقَامَ لَكُمْ means “there is no place for you to remain here” or “you can’t stay here” i.e., referring to the battlefield.

Derivative: عَوْرَةٌ a place of exposure. It literally means a hole in the wall, something that you can penetrate and attack through. It is something that needs to be covered or shielded and it is why the male’s and female’s nakedness is named عَوْرَةٌ.
Derivative: تَلَبَّثُوا from تَلَبَّثَ which means to linger, hang around or remain for a time, to take one’s time and think things through, or to hesitate.
- يُوَلُّونَ الْأَدْبَارَ and any form of وَلَّى الأَدْبار means to turn your backs in the context of fleeing from the battle. It is an expression to mean turning tail and running away from battle.

Derivative: تُمَتَّعُونَ from مَتَّعَ means to be provided with the utilities of life which can refer to the enjoyments and pleasures of life i.e., in this verse it means “you will only be able to enjoy the benefits of this life for a short time in any case (before you inevitably die, are resurrected and judged)”.
Derivative: يَعْصِمُ means to protect and implies holding back and preventing something for the purpose of protection.

Derivative: الْمُعَوِّقِينَ “The hinderers” from عَوَّقَ which means to hinder and place obstacles عَوائِق. It is also used to mean to frustrate the efforts of others and acting to demotivate them carrying the same meaning as ثَبَّطَ – تَثْبيط which is to hold back, hinder and frustrate.
Derivative: الْبَأْسَ which can mean might and power, or harm and injury, refers here to the fighting, Jihad and battle.

Derivative: أَشِحَّةً is the plural of شَحِيح and means the one who engages in شُحٌّ. شُحٌّ (Shuhh) combines the extreme desire for wealth or avarice with stinginess. بُخْل (Bukhl) is stinginess in Arabic and حِرْص (Hirs) is covetousness whilst شُحٌّ (Shuhh) combines the meaning of both.
Derivative: الْخَيْرِ (Goodness) refers to wealth and specifically to the booty.
Derivative: سَلَقُو from سَلَقَ which means to speak beyond set limits and be offensive, like giving someone a lashing with their tongue i.e., words.

Derivative: أُسْوَةٌ means a model and example. مُوَاساة from the same root means to have compassion and feeling for one another and آسي is the doctor that cares for you. أُسْوَةٌ is not just an example or role model like قُدْوَة as it contains the meaning of compassion, mercy and taking care within it, implying a role model that has compassion for you and cares for you.
Derivative: نَحْب is a pledge or vow that you mandate upon yourself.
- قَضَىٰ نَحْبَهُ means to fulfil one’s vow or pledge and also derived from that is to die i.e., the one who pledges their life to Allah and fulfils that by dying in His way.
Derivative: غَيْظِ means the combination of anger, rage and frustration i.e., exasperation.

Derivative: صَيَاصِي is the plural of صِيصِيَةُ and refers to the spurs of a cockerel used for defence or the horns of the bull. From this it has been used to refer to anything that protects and fortifies. From that the word is used for fortresses which are used and built for the purpose of a strong defence.
Derivative: الرُّعْبَ is extreme terror where one is incapable of thinking straight and maintaining composure.

Derivative: أُسَرِّحْ from سَرَّحَ originally refers to sending cattle out to pasture and also means to let go, dismiss or release which means in this context “أُسَرِّحْكُنَّ” to divorce you i.e., the wives of the Prophet ﷺ.
Derivative: يَقْنُتْ from قَنَتَ which means to be submissive and obedient – is a state of readiness and complete willingness to obey.
Derivative: تَخْضَعْنَ from خَضَعَ which means to be submissive and humble. In relation to speech, it refers to softness i.e., to be soft spoken.
Derivative: يَطْمَعَ from طَمَعَ means to have, aspirations, hopes, desires or cravings.
- “An appropriate statement/speech” i.e., only say what is necessary and appropriate to the need and situation and in an appropriate manner.
Derivative: قَرْنَ from قَرَّ. The origin of the word is قَرَرْنَ but the second راء has been omitted. It means to commit yourselves (to your homes) and to not leave them a lot.
Derivative: تَبَرَّجْنَ from تَبَرَّجَ – تَبَرُّجًا which means to beautify yourselves for the public’s eyes that draws attention.
Derivative: الرِّجْسَ is impurity and refers to harm and evil.
Derivative: الْحِكْمَةِ in this verse refers to the Sunnah of the Prophet ﷺ.
Derivative: الْقَانِتِينَ وَالْقَانِتَاتِ are those men and women who are ready, willing and enthusiastic to be obedient.
Derivative: الْخَاشِعِينَ وَالْخَاشِعَاتِ those men and women who are overwhelmed by the fear of Allah.
Derivative: الصَّادِقِينَ وَالصَّادِقَاتِ are those men and women who are truthful and also includes the meaning of the مُصَدِّق the one who affirms a matter, lives by it and is true to it.

in the arabic language when males are mentioned like “المُسْلِمينَ” it includes both males and females, however, this verse mentions the women specifically and separately to the men, thereby placing a special significance to and for them.

- The language used here is very unique: The use of the Ism ul-Faa’il (اِسْمُ الفَاعِل) of الْحَافِظِينَ with the Maf’ool Bihi (مَفْعُول بِهِ) of فُرُوجَهُمْ which provides the meaning of those who are continually and constantly guarding their private parts.

- Like the previous statement in the verse this shares the same uniqueness: The use of the Ism ul-Faa’il (اِسْمُ الفَاعِل) of الذَّاكِرِينَ with the Maf’ool Bihi (مَفْعُول بِهِ) of اللَّهَ which provides the meaning of those who are continually and constantly remembering Allah a lot.
Derivative: وَطَرًا is any important need or aim.
Derivative: زَيْدٌ (Zaid): This is the only time in the Qur’an that a Sahabiy (companion) has been mentioned specifically by his name.

- قَضَى وَطَرَهُ is an expression that he fulfilled or reached his aim, wish or objective i.e., he fulfilled what he set out to do or accomplish and are no longer in need or want for it i.e., this is referring to Zaid’s wish to not continue to remain married to Zainab, may Allah be pleased with them.
#(note: regarding the above and verse 33:37 Ustadh Nouman states that the above means to have intimacy in the bedroom and then focuses on that interpretation to discuss marital intimacy, however, I have referenced the classical dictionaries and the Tafsirs of this verse and the understanding I found is what I have placed above, whilst I have found nothing to support what Ustadh Numan said here. It’s not the first time that he has stated things that do not appear to have a supporting basis for them or are strange but this is the most extreme I have come across so far. Throughout this work I have placed in yellow highlights the opinions as found in the relied upon sources when they contradict or add to what he has said and appear to be stronger and most likely).
- قَدَرًا مَّقْدُورًا refers to a matter a decree that has been decided and there is no escape from it. It’s occurrence is inevitable.

Derivative: أَدْعِيَاءَ are those who are called sons by other than their real biological fathers and includes the reality of adoption, where someone acts as a guardian for a child who is not his own. In pre-Islamic Makkan society these adopted sons were considered to be exactly the same as biological sons.

Derivative: تُظَاهِرُونَ means that you have done ظِهار against your wife which is a transgression and injustice against her. It means to say to the wife: “You are to me like the back of my mother” which carries the meaning that you are like someone who is Haram (prohibited) for me and was uses in pre-Islamic Makkan society to indicate to divorce.
Derivative: جَوْفِ literally means something which is hollow inside and is a cavity. It is used to refer to the chest or torso of the human being in which most of the body’s organs are found.
Derivative: خَاتَمَ means that nothing comes after it. As such it has been translated as the seal or final.

Derivative: تَحِيَّتُهُمْ from حَيَّا – يحيّي – تَحِيّةً in origin means to grant a long life like in the supplication حَيَّاكَ الله may Allah grant you a long life. It also means to greet or give greetings.
- It means by His (Allah’s) mandate and command.
Derivative: فَضْلًا differs from أَجْر, ثَواب and جَزاء which refer to reward and recompense for what you do. فَضْل implies something that is extra and a bonus.

Derivative: أَذَا is originally أَذًى and refers to harm although it encompasses the harm caused by words through slandering, insulting, mocking ridiculing and plotting and not just physical harm. It refers to a cause of upset, distress or making someone uncomfortable.
Derivative: سَرِّحُوهُنَّ from سَرَّحَ which means to let go, release or set free.

Derivative: أُجُورَ (Ujoor) is the plural of أَجْر (Ajr) which means reward or recompense in return for something. Here, Allah uses أُجُورَ to refer to the مَهْر (dowry) implying that it is something that must be paid like a salary.
Derivative: أَفَاءَ what has been given to you as فَيْء (Fai’) which in origin refers to the spoils of war and in general it can mean to give and grant something.

Derivative: تُرْجِي from أَرْجَا – إِرْجاء and it means to delay or defer. Here, it refers to the Messenger ﷺ being permitted to defer time from his wives as he wishes, which is specific to him as it is obligatory for Muslim men to allocate their time equally between their wives. Here, Allah is saying that it is permissible for him ﷺ to allocate his time with his wives as he wishes.

Derivative: إِنَاهُ from أَنَى which means to ripen, to draw near (i.e., time) or to become ready. Here, it refers to the time of the food being ready and means to not show up early to the invitation of the Prophet ﷺ for you to come to eat at his house.

Derivative: سَلِّمُوا is from سَلَّمَ – تَسْليمًا and it has two meanings. The first is to say Salaam to someone and give them salutations and the second is to submit completely and surrender yourself to something. In the first meaning the verse is instructing the believers to send their Salaam upon the Prophet ﷺ. According to the second meaning, it would imply submitting yourselves completely in obedience to the Prophet ﷺ and following his instructions.

يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا صَلُّوا عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيمًا

- “O you who have believed, ask [Allah to confer] blessing upon him and ask [Allah to grant him] peace” can be encapsulated in the well-known supplication: “صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ”.

Derivative: يُؤْذُونَ from آَذَى – يُؤْذِي – إِيذَاء means to cause harm and refers to all forms of harm including the acts of slandering, mocking, ridiculing, backbiting, insulting, plotting and scheming etc.
Derivative: بُهْتَانًا refers to a false accusation and slanderous statement.

Derivative: يُدْنِينَ from أَدْنَى – يُدْنِي – إدناءً and it means to bring something close to another thing. It also means to lower. أَدْنَى with a garment implies to place it over you from above and to let it drop down in a loose manner.
Derivative: أَدْنَى: The verb is used for a second time in the verse to mean closer and in the context, it means closer to what is correct or best.
Derivative: أَوْلَىٰ means to have greater precedence.

- “And his wives are their (i.e., the believers) mothers”: This not only means that they have the honoured standing as being the mothers of the believers but also that they follow the rules of mothers in respect to not being permitted to marry them after the Messenger ﷺ.

Derivative: الْمُرْجِفُونَ literally means those who want to cause convulsions and shake the surroundings. It refers to those who want to cause trouble, controversies and shake up the society. It is used for those who spread lies and rumours.

Derivative: نُغْرِيَنَّ from أَغْرَى – يُغْرِي – إِغْراء which means to incite against. In this Verse: “If those don’t stop what they are doing, We will incite you against them and make them your target to punish and enforce your dominance over.
- “And what will make you know (when the final hour is)?” which affirms that the Messenger ﷺ doesn’t know.
Derivative: وَلِيًّا – Protective friend.
Derivative: وَجِيهًا of a noble and honourable standing.
Derivative: مِيثَاقَ is a contract, agreement or covenant where both parties involved clearly know what they have agreed to in terms of its serious and its terms.

Derivative: سَدِيدًا comes from سَدّ which means a preventative wall or barrier that blocks or barricades. سِداد means that you come up with something that blocks a hole in the wall or plugs something up i.e., it solves the problem and fixes it. سَدِيد when linked to speech means that speech that solves problems. It is a clear, concise and unambiguous speech that deals with the matter at hand appropriately. It is speech that hits the target and produces the best results.
Derivative: جَهُولًا extremely foolish and ignorant. It implies impulsiveness and rashness.
Derivative: ظَلُومًا means extremely wrongdoing and transgressing i.e., against himself.
